title: eriogonum campanulatum subsp. grangerense
intro: Eriogonum campanulatum subsp. grangerense, also known as Granger's buckwheat or Granger's desert buckwheat, is a subspecies of the Eriogonum campanulatum plant, a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Granger's buckwheat is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also sometimes used topically in salves and creams.
